name: reply
title: Discord Reply
description: Send a message to a Discord channel. Supports text, reply threading, file attachments, embeds, and components.

files.description: Absolute file paths to attach. Max 10, 25MB each.

name: react
title: Reaction
description: Add emoji reaction to a message.

name: edit_message
title: Edit Message
description: Edit a bot message. Supports text, embeds, components.

name: download_attachment
title: Download Attachment
description: Download message attachments to local inbox. Returns file paths.

name: fetch
title: Fetch
description: Fetch recent messages from a channel. Returns oldest-first with IDs.

name: schedule_status
title: Schedule Status
description: Show all schedules with next fire time and running state.

name: trigger_schedule
title: Trigger Schedule
description: Manually fire a named schedule immediately.

name: schedule_control
title: Schedule Control
description: Defer or skip a schedule. defer: suppress N minutes. skip_today: suppress rest of day.

name: activate_channel_bridge
title: Activate Channel Bridge
description: Toggle channel bridge. Active: typing indicators, reactions, auto-forward. Inactive: manual tool calls only.

name: reload_config
title: Reload Config
description: Reload config.json and re-register schedules, webhooks, event rules.

name: memory
title: Memory Cycle
description: Run memory operations: cycle2/sleep (promote+dedup), flush, rebuild, prune, cycle1, backfill, status, remember (store fact).

name: recall
title: Recall
description: Past context — prior decisions, preferences, conversation and work history from the memory store. Accepts a natural-language query or an array of queries; an internal agent fans out in parallel. DEFAULT IS ASYNC (bridge-style): returns an `async_...` handle immediately and the answer is collected later via `session_result`. Pass `wait:true` to block inline until the merged answer comes back. Not for external web (use `search`) or codebase files (use `explore`).

query.description: Natural language query, or an array of queries for multi-angle parallel fan-out.

cwd.description: Optional workspace hint forwarded to the recall agent. Absolute path recommended; `~` expansion supported; forward slashes work on Windows/WSL.

wait.description: Defaults to false (async). Pass `wait:true` to block inline until the merged answer comes back; otherwise poll the returned `async_...` handle with `session_result`.

name: search
title: Search
description: External web — live web search, URL scrape, GitHub code/issues/repos. Accepts a natural-language query or an array of queries; include a URL to trigger scrape or mention `owner/repo` for GitHub. An internal agent fans out in parallel and picks the provider per query. DEFAULT IS ASYNC (bridge-style): returns an `async_...` handle immediately and the answer is collected later via `session_result`. Pass `wait:true` to block inline until the merged answer comes back. Not for past context (use `recall`) or codebase files (use `explore`).

query.description: Natural language query, or an array of queries for multi-angle parallel fan-out. Include a URL to trigger scrape; mention `owner/repo` for GitHub code/issues.

cwd.description: Optional workspace hint forwarded to the search agent. Rarely needed — only when the query is ambiguous about which repo context applies.

wait.description: Defaults to false (async). Pass `wait:true` to block inline until the merged answer comes back; otherwise poll the returned `async_...` handle with `session_result`.

name: explore
title: Explore
description: Internal codebase — local filesystem file and code search. Accepts a natural-language query or an array of queries; an internal explorer agent fans out `glob` / `grep` / `read` / `multi_read` in parallel and returns a synthesized answer with concrete file paths. DEFAULT IS ASYNC (bridge-style): returns an `async_...` handle immediately and the answer is collected later via `session_result`. Pass `wait:true` to block inline. Root auto-detection picks between the launch workspace and `~/.claude`; pass `cwd` to override. Not for past context (use `recall`) or external web (use `search`).

query.description: Natural language query, or an array of queries for multi-angle parallel fan-out.

cwd.description: Override the search root. Absolute path recommended; `~` expansion supported; forward slashes work on Windows/WSL. When omitted, the server auto-picks between the launch workspace (`cwd`) and `~/.claude` based on the query.

wait.description: Defaults to false (async). Pass `wait:true` to block inline until the merged answer comes back; otherwise poll the returned `async_...` handle with `session_result`.

name: session_result
title: Session Result
description: Poll a background dispatch started via `recall` / `search` / `explore` with `wait:false`. Pass the `async_...` id returned by the original call. Returns the merged answer once the dispatch is done, a short `still running` message while in flight, or an error description on failure. Not for regular Pool B / Pool C session ids — those are managed internally.

id.description: The `async_...` handle returned by the original wait:false dispatch.

name: read
title: Read
description: Read a file from disk with cat -n line-numbered output, byte-size cap, and optional offset/limit windowing.

name: edit
title: Edit
description: Replace one unique occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string` in a file. `old_string` must match exactly once.

name: write
title: Write
description: Create or overwrite a file with the provided content.

name: bash
title: Bash
description: Execute a shell command with a configurable timeout. A small blocked-pattern safety list rejects `rm -rf /`, `git push --force`, `format c:`, etc.

name: grep
title: Grep
description: ripgrep-backed content search. `pattern` and `glob` both accept a string or an array of strings (OR-joined in one invocation). Output modes: `files_with_matches` (default), `content`, `count`; `head_limit` caps the return set.

name: glob
title: Glob
description: File name / path search via ripgrep --files. `pattern` accepts a string or an array of globs (OR-joined in one invocation).

name: multi_edit
title: Multi Edit
description: Apply several ordered replacements to ONE file in a single call. Edits are chained in memory first; if any `old_string` fails to match uniquely (or at all) the file is left untouched. `replace_all:true` per entry drops the uniqueness requirement.

name: multi_read
title: Multi Read
description: Read several files in one call. Each entry in `reads` is { path, offset?, limit? } with the same semantics as a single read. Returns `### <path>` delimited sections; per-file errors appear inline and do not abort the batch.

path.description: Absolute file path (relative paths resolve against the plugin server cwd).

name: batch_edit
title: Batch Edit
description: Apply edits across several files in one call. Each entry in `edits` is { path, old_string, new_string } with the same unique-match rule as a single edit. Per-entry failures are reported inline (`FAIL <path>: <reason>`) and do not abort the batch.

old_string.description: Exact text to find (must be unique in the file).

name: create_session
description: Create external AI session with tool access. Auto-injects context. Use preset: full/readonly/mcp. Use agent: Worker/Reviewer.

provider.description: openai, anthropic, gemini, groq, openrouter, xai, copilot, ollama, lmstudio, local

name: list_sessions
description: List active orchestrator sessions.

name: close_session
description: Close an orchestrator session.

name: list_models
description: List available models from all providers.

name: bridge
title: Bridge to External Model
description: Delegate one turn of work to an external agent by role. Role maps to a preset via user-workflow.json (e.g. worker→OPUS XHIGH, reviewer→GPT5.4). Use this to hand off code, research, debug, review, or test work — the lead does not do these directly.

prompt.description: The task instruction for the agent.

role.description: Agent role as defined in user-workflow.json. Default roles: worker, researcher, reviewer, debugger, tester. Users may customize — check user-workflow.json for the actual set.

preset.description: Advanced: explicit preset name (bypass role mapping).

context.description: Extra context appended to the prompt.

file.description: Read prompt from a file path.
